supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on third-party technology providers including AWS for hosting online betting and gaming offerings, third-party sports data providers for sportsbook operations, and outsourced IT support vendors
supply_chain.risks: Third-party service provider failures or interruptions could adversely affect business, cybersecurity risks from outsourced IT vendors (Data Incident from social engineering attack on outsourced IT support vendor), dependency on third-party platforms for distribution of mobile apps, reliance on third-party sports data providers for accurate information

regulatory_risks: Gaming licenses subject to renewal and regulatory approval - failure to maintain or renew licenses would have material adverse effect
Gaming authorities may require holders of securities to file applications and be found suitable - unsuitable persons could trigger sanctions including loss of gaming approvals
Officers, directors and key employees subject to suitability findings - may be required to terminate employment if unsuitable
May require gaming authority approval to issue securities, incur debt and undertake financing activities
Financing counterparties including lenders subject to licensing and approval procedures in various jurisdictions
Subject to restrictions on indoor tobacco smoking - additional restrictions could decrease gaming revenue
FINCEN regulations require reporting of currency transactions exceeding $10,000 and suspicious activity - substantial penalties for non-compliance
FINCEN has increased focus on gaming companies for Bank Secrecy Act and Anti-Money Laundering compliance
IRS regulations require reporting of certain gambling winnings via Form W-2G - changes could increase compliance costs

Subject to various federal, state and local laws including alcoholic beverage restrictions, food service, environmental matters, employment practices, currency transactions, taxation, zoning and building codes, marketing and advertising
Gaming companies subject to significant taxes and fees in addition to normal income taxes - subject to increase at any time
Large number of state and local governments with budget deficits may seek to fund deficits with new or increased gaming taxes and property taxes
Privacy laws and regulations subject to frequent changes and vary by jurisdiction - may limit ability to market to customers

Expansion of online betting and gaming dependent on adoption of regulations permitting such activities in new jurisdictions - terms of regulations including restrictions, tax rates and license fees uncertain
May not be able to obtain required licenses for online betting and gaming in new jurisdictions
Pillar Two global minimum tax framework - various countries enacting legislation, potential future impacts uncertain

sanction_risks: Chinese government has taken steps to prohibit transfer of cash for payment of gaming debts - may reduce collectability of gaming debts from players from China
